matched_class_path: gene modification or gain of function
primary_variables.matched_definition: Addition of bases or sequence cassette (for example donor insertion, tag insertion, transposon insertion).
evidence: we generated HEK-293T cells stably expressing various human genes fused with SFB triple tags (S protein, FLAG, and streptavidin-binding peptide tags)

Fourteen genes encoding proteins in ferroptosis pathways were selected for this study, including genes involved in (1) PUFA-PL synthesis and peroxidation (ACSL4, LPCAT3, ALOX12, ALOX15, PEBP1), (2) iron metabolism (NCOA4), and (3) ferroptosis defense (SLC7A11, GCLC, GCLM, GSS, GPX4, GCH1, FSP1, DHODH)

attributes.agent: SFB-tagged bait proteins (ACSL4, LPCAT3, ALOX12, ALOX15, PEBP1, NCOA4, SLC7A11, GCLC, GCLM, GSS, GPX4, GCH1, FSP1, DHODH)
evidence: Fourteen genes encoding proteins in ferroptosis pathways were selected for this study, including genes involved in (1) PUFA-PL synthesis and peroxidation (ACSL4, LPCAT3, ALOX12, ALOX15, PEBP1), (2) iron metabolism (NCOA4), and (3) ferroptosis defense (SLC7A11, GCLC, GCLM, GSS, GPX4, GCH1, FSP1, DHODH)

replicate_hints: We performed two replicates for each bait protein

evidence: We applied SAINTexpress to filter the list by comparison to the controls (296 tandem affinity purification-MS experiments using different bait genes from our previous publications as well as unpublished studies; we selected those genes that have not been reported to have any known function directly related to ferroptosis pathways).

targets_and_enrichments.ontology_label: Affinity purification coupled with mass spectrometry proteomics

base_candidates_ranked.reason: The experiment used data-dependent acquisition (DDA) on a Q Exactive HF system with label-free quantification (LFQ) logic, as evidenced by the use of SAINTexpress for comparing bait samples against controls.

modifier_candidates_ranked.reason: The study explicitly describes tandem affinity purification (TAP) using SFB-tagged proteins (S-protein, FLAG, and streptavidin-binding peptide) to identify interacting proteins.
